data.description:
## Why
The code-review gate cast ZERO 'BLOCK — finding' votes across 162 changes since the code-v2 impact model landed, while 76% of changes carried advisories and at least three carried confirmed must-fix findings (#529 impact 0.0 — proved out on main within the hour; #518 importlib code-execution at 0.54; #539 correctness at 0.60). Observed impact distribution clusters at {0, 0.32, 0.36, 0.54, 0.60} with an apparent 0.60 ceiling — the 0.95 default threshold is unreachable. Before enabling blocking we need the empirically-correct threshold, not a guess: the operator's decision rule is the crossover point at which a finding is MORE LIKELY correctly-blocking than false-blocking (0.6 is the prior/starting hypothesis).

## What
- Assemble the post-2026-07-08 code-v2 corpus: the ~149 deduplicated REVIEW_RESULT code-review finding records in the ticket store (.tickets-tracker) joined to their Gerrit changes and post-merge history (follow-up fix commits, red-main incidents).
- Adjudicate each finding with impact >= 0.3 as block-worthy vs not (blind to its score where feasible), mirroring the b463 plan-review methodology (manual scoring of the would-block slice; Sonnet-scored simulation with held-out adjudication acceptable for scale).
- Compute precision(block-worthy | impact >= t) across candidate thresholds t in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.54, 0.6}; select the SMALLEST t where precision > 0.5, tie-broken toward fewer false blocks. Explicitly analyze the 0.54 band (the #518 security findings) and whether the 0.60 ceiling means t=0.6 only ever catches max-impact findings.
- Run docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py + the version-segmented replay (docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py --impact-model-version code-v2) per ADR 0036 — the mandated evidence gate before any threshold-down.

## Scope
Analysis + a written threshold recommendation recorded on this ticket (with the adjudication table as an artifact under docs/experiments/); NO routing/config change here (that is the sibling enablement story).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The adjudicated corpus (finding id, impact, adjudication, evidence) is committed under docs/experiments/ with the derivation script or notebook.
- [ ] The chosen threshold and its precision-crossover justification are recorded in a comment on this ticket, including the 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses.
- [ ] The ADR-0036 A/B gate result (HIGH<->NIT separation vs baseline) is recorded and passes.
- [ ] make lint passes on the committed artifacts.
- [ ] make test passes on the committed artifacts.
data.title: Derive the code-review block threshold from the adjudicated code-v2 finding distribution
